scenario_content:
[Issue Title]: A lot of Emojis are missing from Emoji drawer and Emoji ordering and categorization is incorrect

[Post 1]:
Hello,
a lot of Emojis are missing from the TextSecure Emoji drawer.
When I say a lot of Emojis are missing, then I am not referring to the fact that TextSecure is using an outdated Emoji set.
What I mean is that they are actually missing and should be there even in the oudated Emoji set.
I also noticed that the Emoji ordering and categorization is incorrect.
The Emojis in the categories have a different ordering than they should have and some Emojis are placed in the wrong categories.
The issue seems to be caused by the actual source files, see:
https://github.com/WhisperSystems/TextSecure/blob/master/assets/emoji_0_wrapped.png
https://github.com/WhisperSystems/TextSecure/blob/master/assets/emoji_1_wrapped.png
https://github.com/WhisperSystems/TextSecure/blob/master/assets/emoji_2_wrapped.png
https://github.com/WhisperSystems/TextSecure/blob/master/assets/emoji_3_wrapped.png
https://github.com/WhisperSystems/TextSecure/blob/master/assets/emoji_4_wrapped.png
Because those source files exhibit the same issues as mentioned above.
Here is a list of the Emojis which should be available in the outdated set and the list also shows the expected ordering and categorization of the Emojis, see (you can toggle between the different categories by clicking on the smiley/flower/bell/car/symbols icons in the upper right corner above the list):
http://emoji.codes/family
But please note that Apple already seems to have introduced/adopted a new Emoji category / ordering system in their latest OS updates, see:

http://blog.emojipedia.org/apple-2015-emoji-changelog-ios-os-x
Emoji Categories
Prepare to re-learn the position of your favorite emojis,
as they have all changed. New categories have been introduced,
and emojis have been re-arranged within the existing categories as well.
The new categories are:
� People
� Nature
� Food & Drink
� Celebration
� Activity
� Travel & Places
� Objects & Symbols
Essentially, many emojis that were previously in the People or Objects categories
may now be in one of the new, more-specific categories. Objects & Symbols is now
a catch-all for the more obscure remaining characters.

So, maybe it would make sense to adopt that new system.
So, could you please add all the missing Emojis (and ideally update to the new set while being at it) and correct the ordering of the Emojis either to the traditional system or to the new system that Apple is already using?
It would be much appreciated.
Regards

[Post 2]:
PS:
Seems like Emojipedia has already adopted the new categories that Apple is using now, so you can see the categories and the Emojis in them over there:
http://emojipedia.org/
Regards

[Post 3]:
I am also experiencing this on android (5.0.2). In addition to the missing emoji, emoji are showing up different in the keyboard and in the text box, for example the hairy heart is hairy on the keyboard and yellow in the text box. Additionally I now don't have access to my keyboard's emoji drawer since the built in textsecure emoji drawer seems to be overriding it.

[Post 4]:
@SwartzCr thats a different issue (#2936)

[Post 5]:
@SwartzCr wrote:
In addition to the missing emoji, emoji are showing up different in the keyboard and in the text box, for example the hairy heart is hairy on the keyboard and yellow in the text box

See:
#2894
and:
#2936

@SwartzCr wrote:
Additionally I now don't have access to my keyboard's emoji drawer since the built in textsecure emoji drawer seems to be overriding it.

Actually it doesn't. Previously TextSecure was replacing the Enter key with a smiley key.
It is no longer doing this, since you are supposed to use the built-in Emoji drawer from TextSecure now (via the Emoji drawer toggle button on the left side of the message input box).
BUT:
You should still be able to access the Emoji keyboard of your main keyboard via the traditional way.
And that is by simply pressing and holding the Enter key.
Regards

[Post 6]:
You are correct, I updated my phone and text secure at the same time, so I thought that the smiley-replacing-enter-key was part of my OS not textsecure, I can in fact still access the emoji keyboard by long pressing on the enter key. (but the emoji that show on my keyboard and the emoji that show when I click the smiley in the text box are different)

[Post 7]:
blocked on #3153
system_prompt:
你是一位经验丰富的软件工程师和项目管理者，专注于软件系统中的价值风险分析。
你的任务是分析给定的GitHub Issue讨论内容，识别其中涉及的人类价值关切。

价值风险是指软件系统可能对用户或利益相关者的重要价值（如隐私、安全、公平性、可用性等）产生负面影响的潜在问题。
Issue讨论中可能包含用户对软件行为的不满、功能请求、Bug报告等，这些内容可能隐含对某些价值的关切。
user_prompt:

## 价值模型参考
以下是可能涉及的价值类型及其定义：
### 人类价值主题 (L2)
- **HV1 (Conformity)**: Adhering to rules, regulations, guidelines, or commonly accepted standards during application use or development.
- **HV2 (Pleasure)**: Providing enjoyment and satisfaction to users when using the application.
- **HV3 (Dignity)**: Maintaining users’ sense of dignity, respect, and personal worth when interacting with the application.
- **HV4 (Inclusiveness)**: Enabling people from different backgrounds, languages, cultures, or knowledge levels to use the application.
- **HV5 (Sense_of_Belonging)**: Supporting users’ feeling of being connected to a group or community through the application.
- **HV6 (Freedom)**: Providing users with options and choices, and avoiding forced dependencies on specific services or workflows.
- **HV7 (Independence)**: Reducing dependency on third-party services or external systems.
- **HV8 (Wealth)**: Considering the monetary or economic cost associated with using the application.
- **HV9 (Privacy)**: Protecting users’ personal information and addressing concerns about data collection or tracking.
- **HV10 (Security)**: Protecting users from external threats or attacks when using the application.

### 系统价值主题 (L3)
- **SV1 (Trust)**: Compelling users to believe that an application is harmless.
- **SV2 (Correctness)**: Ensuring that the application provides expected information and behavior.
- **SV3 (Compatibility)**: Supporting a range of user devices and operating systems.
- **SV4 (Portability)**: Allowing the application to be migrated to other devices.
- **SV5 (Reliability)**: Reducing the occurrence of application failures.
- **SV6 (Efficiency)**: Reducing the time and effort required to perform tasks within the application.
- **SV7 (Energy_Preservation)**: Reducing power or energy consumption during application use.
- **SV8 (Usability)**: Improving the ease of use of the application.
- **SV9 (Accessibility)**: Improving access to information or functionality, particularly for users with special needs.
- **SV10 (Longevity)**: Ensuring prolonged availability of the application in the future.

## 任务
请你以一个软件工程师和项目管理者的视角，回答以下两个问题：

（1）上述Issue讨论是否涉及价值风险？请回答"是"或"否"。

（2）如果涉及价值风险，可能涉及哪些价值风险？请从上述价值模型中选择相关的价值类型，并说明原因。

## 输出格式
请严格按照以下JSON格式输出：
```json
{
  "has_value_risk": true/false,
  "identified_values": [
    {
      "value_id": "价值ID（如HV1, SV1等）",
      "value_name": "价值名称",
      "confidence": 0.0-1.0之间的置信度,
      "reasoning": "识别该价值风险的理由"
    }
  ],
  "overall_analysis": "整体分析说明"
}
```
